name: Frank Nguyen
technical_background: No formal coding experience. Strong HRIS and reporting skills (Workday, ADP). Advanced Excel for workforce reporting. Familiar with HR compliance frameworks and employment law. Has used AI-powered resume screening tools as an end user but did not configure or evaluate them.

current_profile.summary: HR Manager with 15+ years of experience across recruiting, HR operations, employee relations, and compliance at a Fortune 500 manufacturing company. Manages a team of 6 HR professionals and oversees the full employee lifecycle. Strong stakeholder management skills — regularly partners with business unit leaders and presents workforce metrics to executive leadership. Comfortable with HRIS systems (Workday, ADP) and advanced Excel for workforce reporting (headcount, turnover, compensation analysis). Has used AI-powered resume screening tools (HireVue, Pymetrics) as an end user in the recruiting process but has no understanding of how the models work, potential biases, or evaluation criteria. Uses ChatGPT occasionally for drafting job descriptions and employee communications. No coding or analytics modeling experience.
technical_skills: Excel reporting (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, workforce dashboards)
HR compliance and employment law (FLSA, FMLA, ADA, EEO)
Employee engagement survey design and analysis
current_profile.ai_experience: Has used AI-powered resume screening tools (HireVue, Pymetrics) as a recruiter/evaluator in the hiring process but did not configure the models and has no understanding of how they work, potential biases, or fairness metrics. Uses ChatGPT occasionally for drafting job descriptions, employee communications, and summarizing policy documents. Attended an HR Technology conference with sessions on AI in hiring and workforce analytics. No analytics modeling, coding, or data science experience.

learning_intent: Wants to build evidence-based HR strategy using analytics and AI: attrition prediction, hiring funnel optimization, engagement analysis, and compensation benchmarking. Goal: become a people analytics leader who can implement responsible AI in hiring and workforce decisions, evaluate AI-powered HR tools for fairness and compliance, and build a data-driven HR culture. Wants to ensure any AI used in hiring decisions is transparent, fair, and compliant with EEOC and emerging AI employment regulations.

target_jd.title: People Analytics Lead / HR Analytics Manager
requirements: Own workforce analytics strategy: build dashboards and predictive models for attrition, engagement, and performance
Analyze engagement and retention drivers; recommend evidence-based interventions to leadership
Evaluate and govern AI tools used in hiring for fairness, bias, and legal compliance (EEOC, state AI laws)
Communicate data insights and influence executive decisions with compelling data storytelling
Build predictive models for hiring funnel optimization and quality-of-hire measurement
Design A/B tests for HR program effectiveness (onboarding, training, engagement initiatives)
Ensure compliance with AI employment regulations and document model governance practices
Partner with IT and data engineering to build reliable HR data pipelines and reporting infrastructure
Develop people analytics capability roadmap and train HR team on data-driven decision making
Monitor AI-powered HR tools for disparate impact, drift, and performance degradation

expected_skill_gaps.description: SQL fundamentals for querying HRIS data warehouses. Python basics for analytics and building simple predictive models.
Bias recognition and evaluation of AI outputs and tools used in HR decisions. Recognizing AI-generated content in candidate applications.
AI risk framing for hiring decisions, policy-to-controls mapping, data minimization, and documentation standards for AI in employment.
Evaluating AI screening tools for accuracy and fairness, monitoring for disparate impact and model drift, and understanding evaluation types.
Data storytelling with HR analytics, explaining AI limitations to leadership and candidates, and writing AI project proposals.
HR-specific AI considerations: hiring fairness, adverse impact testing, and responsible use of AI in workforce decisions.
Understanding AI fundamentals, model limitations, and bias/fairness concepts critical for evaluating AI in HR decisions.
Data leakage prevention and privacy considerations for employee data used in AI analytics.
